# Tools & Utilities

## Web & Data Tools

### 📊 Data Input Tool

**Repository**: [data_input_tool](https://github.com/Utzi1/data_input_tool)

An interactive web-based tool for structured data collection and entry.

**Features**:
- User-friendly interface
- Data validation
- Export functionality
- Real-time feedback

### 📚 Interactive Tutorials

**Repository**: [interactive-tutorials](https://github.com/Utzi1/interactive-tutorials)

Educational content with embedded interactive examples.

**Topics Covered**:
- Programming basics
- Data analysis workflows
- Visualization techniques

## Development & Configuration

### 🎨 Neovim Configuration

**Repository**: [nvim_config](https://github.com/Utzi1/nvim_config)

A full-featured Neovim setup optimized for productivity.

**Includes**:
- Language server integration
- Git integration
- Telescope fuzzy finder
- Treesitter syntax highlighting
- Custom keybindings

### 🔧 Vim Configuration

**Repository**: [dotvim](https://github.com/Utzi1/dotvim)

Traditional vim configuration for compatibility and portability.

### 📝 Neovim Documentation

**Repository**: [neovim](https://github.com/Utzi1/neovim)

Personal documentation and notes about Neovim usage.

## Project Organization Tools

### 📋 Arbeitsplan (Work Plan)

**Repository**: [Arbeitsplan](https://github.com/Utzi1/Arbeitsplan)

Tools and templates for project planning and task management.

### 📌 Master Project 2023

**Repository**: [Master_Project_2023](https://github.com/Utzi1/Master_Project_2023)

Documentation and tools for academic project management.

## Utility Libraries

### ⚙️ Generic Utilities

- Custom helper functions
- Data processing pipelines
- Automation scripts

### 🔗 Integration Tools

- GitHub workflow integrations
- Data pipeline automation
- Processing scripts

## Setup Instructions

Most tools have their own README files. To get started:

```bash
git clone https://github.com/Utzi1/[repository-name]
cd [repository-name]
# Follow the project-specific README
```

## Contributing

Many of these tools are open to improvements. If you have ideas or find bugs, feel free to:
1. Open an issue
2. Fork the repository
3. Submit a pull request
